Space Business Innovation Centre (SBIC) Noordwijk supports space-business entrepreneurs in turning innovative space-based ideas into successful, scalable companies. We help startups validate markets, develop strong value propositions, and build commercially viable business models using space technologies and data — including, where relevant, Earth Observation (EO) and satellite data for downstream applications. Our support covers commercial and technical readiness, access to industry and ESA networks, and practical guidance on navigating the European space ecosystem.
We offer hands-on support with go-to-market strategy, customer and partner development, competitive positioning, and international business growth. We also prepare founders for investment through pitch training, business and financial planning, and introductions to ESA programmes, partners, and investors. Alongside this, founders receive personalised coaching to build confidence, sharpen decision-making, and accelerate their entrepreneurial journey.
By connecting startups to the right expertise, partners, and opportunities, SBIC Noordwijk helps space-driven ventures validate faster, scale smarter, and achieve commercial success in Europe’s growing space economy.

DeepTech2Scale´s mission is empowering entrepreneurs, startups, scale‑ups, spin‑offs, new ventures and every stakeholder engaged in building deep‑technology enterprises. We recognise the unique challenge of bringing highly innovative companies to market, firms developing game‑changing, cutting‑edge technologies grounded in scientific research, advanced engineering and robust innovation. To support this, we offer a suite of curated strategic, tactical and operational services spanning Scalability and Exploitation advisory and support (Go‑To‑Market planning, commercialisation, business development, sales, etc). Our focus is squarely on enabling the leap from scientific/engineering breakthrough to real‑world impact, market adoption and long‑term scalability.
Exploitation & Path‑to‑Market Service: from Technology Readiness Level (TRL) 1 through TRL 9, we help you develop a comprehensive, market‑oriented strategy for exploiting R&D outcomes. We start at project inception by aligning innovation’s knowledge assets and anticipated use cases, and then build a tailored “path to market” journey that ensures exploitation, not just invention. Scalability is embedded throughout: we plan not only for initial entry, but for replicable deployment, growth and sustainable market expansion. Our aim is to turn scientific and technological advances into scalable, impactful products, services or platforms that succeed in the marketplace.

I offer strategic and hands-on guidance to organisations and startups developing innovative solutions, helping them move from concept to market adoption with clarity and measurable results.
I collaborate with teams during key stages such as product validation, market entry and early expansion, ensuring alignment and well-defined milestones. I translate technology into concrete market opportunities, identifying differential advantages from an MVP and focusing on specific niches to engage early adopters. I conduct market opportunity and competitive landscape analyses, and support the design of sales funnels and playbooks to guide early customer acquisition. Together, we refine business models, design go-to-market approaches and establish the foundations for sustainable scalability.
I support teams in shaping realistic sales projections and financial scenarios adapted to different strategic paths and market dynamics, and in developing credible investment and exploitation plans. I also help secure public funding through European innovation programmes such as Horizon Europe, the EIC and Pre-Accelerator initiatives, guiding the preparation of competitive proposals. In addition, I deliver investment readiness services, supporting the design and execution of fundraising strategies with venture capital funds and other private investors.
I tailor my involvement to each organisation’s context, from intensive hands-on collaboration when facing near-term objectives or deadlines, to long-term advisory focused on positioning, capability building and sustainable impact. My aim is to provide the right balance between strategic perspective and operational execution, ensuring tangible progress at every stage.

1. Sustainability Advisory
Consulting services to assess the social, environmental, and economic impacts of corporate activities. We support the integration of sustainability principles into business strategy, the definition of priorities, and the alignment with international standards and regulatory requirements.
2. Life Cycle Assessment (LCA)
Comprehensive evaluation of a product’s environmental impact across its entire life cycle, from raw material extraction and production to use and end-of-life.
3. Carbon Footprint
Measurement and management of greenhouse gas emissions, with tailored strategies to reduce climate impact and support corporate decarbonization objectives.
4. ESG Reporting & Assessment
Preparation of sustainability and ESG reports in compliance with CSRD and ESRS standards. The service is supported by our proprietary digital technology, enabling structured data collection, performance analysis, ESG rating evaluation, and the development of concise ESG disclosures (ESG Express) for organizations requiring streamlined or first-time reporting.
5. Funding and Incentives for Sustainable and Innovative Projects
Support in identifying, applying for, and managing public funding and incentive schemes dedicated to sustainability and innovation. The service covers opportunity scouting, proposal development, project structuring, and compliant reporting, delivered through a tailored advisory approach.
6. Innovation Funding & Incentives 
Support in identifying and managing public funding and incentive schemes for innovation and sustainability projects, including opportunity scouting, proposal preparation, and reporting.

column5:
Space4Good is a team of more than twenty specialists working at the intersection of earth observation, environmental science and data analytics. Since 2017 we have completed more than seventy assignments in over eighty countries, working with startups, scaleups and large corporates. Much of our work focuses on helping organisations build and operate solutions that rely on downstream satellite data, predictive modelling and environmental monitoring. This experience allows us to support SMEs at different stages of developing and deploying their green solutions.
For SMEs at the ideation stage, we help explore how satellite data can strengthen or enable new products and services. This includes identifying relevant datasets, shaping technical concepts, running feasibility assessments and outlining prototype architectures that use Earth observation or predictive analytics.
For SMEs that are ready to scale, we provide geospatial analysis, algorithm development, environmental baselining and monitoring design. Our team can refine early prototypes, validate models, prepare training data, integrate new satellite sources and develop site-specific monitoring pipelines. We also support the design and testing of early warning indicators and nature-risk metrics.
For commercialisation, we help SMEs turn their concepts into reliable, operational services. This includes setting up automated data pipelines, improving performance and accuracy, building clear user-facing insights and supporting field validation. We also help translate geospatial analysis into decision-ready outputs for customers, investors or regulators, and advise on incorporating environmental reporting features aligned with emerging standards.
Besides our technical support, we also have business developers who help SMEs with the market-oriented elements of their projects. They support market and competition studies, the preparation of early business models, market–product fit validation and early-stage business planning. When SMEs move closer to commercialisation, our team can assist with partnership development and client outreach, helping them position their solution and connect with the right stakeholders. This complements our technical work and ensures that new green solutions are grounded in real market needs.

Services Offered as External Service Provider for FIERCE Programme
Comprehensive Technical Services for Space-Enabled Green Innovation
Building Blocks offers end-to-end technical services to support SMEs funded under the FIERCE programme's Ideation, Scale-up, and Commercialisation Vouchers in integrating downstream space data into sustainable business solutions.​
Software Development & System Integration
Our technical expertise encompasses the full development lifecycle for space-enabled applications, including custom software architecture design, API development for satellite data integration, cloud-based platform development using scalable technologies (Supabase, AWS), and enterprise-grade system integration connecting Earth Observation (EO) data streams with existing business processes. We specialize in building robust SaaS solutions that transform raw satellite data into actionable business intelligence.​
AI/ML Implementation for Space Data Analytics
We provide advanced artificial intelligence and machine learning services specifically tailored for downstream space applications, including development of predictive analytics models using satellite imagery, automated data processing pipelines for environmental monitoring, computer vision algorithms for EO data interpretation, and real-time decision support systems leveraging space-based insights. Our AI expertise enables SMEs to extract maximum value from complex satellite datasets for circularity, resource efficiency, and environmental impact reduction.​
Enterprise System Development
We deliver inventory management systems enhanced with satellite-based supply chain monitoring, fleet control software integrated with GPS/GNSS positioning data, stock management solutions incorporating space-based logistics optimization, and business process automation connecting space data to operational workflows. These solutions help SMEs reduce their environmental footprint through data-driven operational efficiency.​
Technical Architecture & Scalability Solutions
We design and implement scalable technical architectures using edge computing for distributed space data processing, database optimization for handling high-volume satellite data streams, microservices architecture for modular space-enabled applications, and security frameworks ensuring data integrity and compliance. Our architectural approach ensures solutions can grow from proof-of-concept to commercial deployment.​
Prototype Development & MVP Services
For SMEs in early ideation phases, we accelerate concept validation through rapid prototyping of space-enabled features, proof-of-concept development demonstrating technical feasibility, MVP development for market testing, and iterative development cycles incorporating user feedback. This approach aligns perfectly with FIERCE's goal of helping SMEs design, upscale, and commercialize green space-enabled solutions.​
Integration & Data Management
We provide seamless integration services connecting multiple space data sources (Copernicus, commercial satellites), data transformation and normalization services, real-time data pipeline development, and API gateway implementation for downstream service delivery. Our solutions ensure SMEs can efficiently access and utilize diverse satellite data streams for environmental applications.​

EcoLoop provides specialized environmental consulting services to support SMEs funded under the FIERCE programme in integrating sustainability, life cycle thinking, and environmental performance improvement into their business models. As an External Service Provider, EcoLoop offers expertise in Life Cycle Assessment (LCA), environmental impact measurement, supply chain analysis, and sustainability strategy development to assist companies at different stages of the programme.
For Ideation Vouchers:
EcoLoop supports SMEs in exploring social and environmental innovation opportunities through lifecycle thinking, preliminary environmental assessments, materiality analysis, and definition of sustainability priorities. Services include high-level Carbon Footprint pre-assessments, identification of environmental hotspots, and guidance on integrating sustainability into early-stage business concepts.
For Scale-up Vouchers:
EcoLoop provides in-depth Life Cycle Assessments (ISO 14040/44), Product and Organizational Carbon Footprints (ISO 14067, ISO 14064), Environmental Product Declarations (EPD), and advanced supply chain environmental analyses. These services help SMEs quantify impacts, optimize processes, select lower-impact materials, and identify reduction strategies aligned with market and regulatory requirements.
For Commercialisation Vouchers:
EcoLoop assists SMEs in transforming environmental performance into market value by supporting environmental communication, sustainability reporting, EPD verification processes, and the development of data-driven messaging for clients, investors, and supply chain partners. EcoLoop also supports the integration of digital tools (IoT-based monitoring, data analysis) to improve transparency and product differentiation.

column5:
LLcloud® (https://www.llcloud.eu/) - the multi-sensor cloud, is one of the few platforms in the world that allows multi-scale multi-sensor Earth Observation (EO), enabling seamless integration and joint analysis of EO, IoT, and wearable sensor data. The platform offers automated data uploaders from smartphone sensors (via the Phyphox and Strava apps), smartwatches, geo-tagged photos (via the photoSpot app, fully integrated with LLcloud), GoPro cameras, Air Quality (AQ) and other IoT sensors, Copernicus and other commercial satellites and drones, etc, and various software modules to jointly visualise and analyse different combinations of such multi-sensor data. LLcloud® has been used by over 20 clients and over 30 research pilots in downstream applications ranging from monitoring city infrastructure for planning and insurance services, air quality control and predictions in cities, climate adaptation and urban heat navigation, placemaking and city tour creation, location tracking in the mountains and in GNSS denied areas, terrain mapping, smart agriculture, etc. We can offer both the LLcloud platform as a service to allow selected FIERCE SMEs to combine and jointly analyse satellite data with other (aerial or ground) sensor data or provide fused data to them for their use case and application. 
In addition, for over 15 years, LLcloud's CEO and co-founder Dr Stavri Nikolov has been a regular mentor of startups in the Galileo Incubation Programme (formerly E-GNSS Accelerator), the Copernicus Accelerator, Copernicus hackathons, and CASSINI hackathons. He is also the founding director of Digital Spaces Living Lab (https://www.digitalspaces.info/) - one of the leading living labs in Europe, which has provided technology and business support and incubated more than 25 startups from across Europe and beyond and has conducted over 45 large-scale technology trials of novel products and services, many of which downstream applications using satellite and other data.

Creative Space is experience in developing GIS-based software solutions using Earth Observation (Sentinel2, Landsat) data for urban development and agriculture, including the monitoring of Urban Heat Islands.  We integrate historical and forecast weather and climate data (MeteoRomania Open Data, cds.copernicus.eu) with ground truth.  
We have delivered a toolkit for agriculutral inputs/outputs management for the Foundation Efficient Resources and Sustainable Agriculture in Romania as on-demand software, and we currently research the suitability of innovative crops on Romanian soil (Sweet potatoes, peanuts, cowpeas) through out exocrop.indecosoft.ro project based on climatic and weather historical and forcast data.
Wh have developed tools for Urban Heat Islands detection and LST enhancement up to 10m resolution) through integrate.indecosoft.ro as a research partner.
We can provide data processing services (on demand), software development including MVP, proof of concept or taylored solutions.  Licensing options varry from full ownership for the end-user, to shared or SaaS models.

column5:
Baltic Satellite Service (BSS) provides satellite and ML based forest and land-use intelligence through its cloud-native GeoHub platform, supporting SMEs in forestry, agriculture, utility, bioeconomy, and environmental services. GeoHub integrates, processes, and publishes EO, drone, LiDAR data, and other spatial data, delivers predictive models and offers ready-to-use dashboards via interoperable web and mobile interfaces. BSS also develops tailored applications and delivers specialized training in ML development, remote sensing, EO workflows, and data-driven decision-making.
BSS is excited to lead, support others and demonstrate how satellite data can be integrated into real-world sustainable business workflows by solving concrete operational problems and combining all additional datasets needed for reliable decision support. For instance, nature-risk assessment and mapping across agricultural or forest land portfolios, facility-level environmental monitoring, and compliance checks for fertilization and other regulatory standards—all showing how space data can directly address end-user needs and support greener, more efficient management.
Services BSS Offers to FIERCE Beneficiaries:
1) Technical Feasibility & Proof-of-Concept
Assessment of how EO and ML can solve specific forestry or agriculture challenges—such as disturbance detection, biomass estimation, operational planning, or nature-risk monitoring. Prototypes are quickly validated using satellite, drone, LiDAR, IoT, other spatial data sets on real forest stands or agricultural fields.
2) MVP Development for Forest & Agriculture Applications
Development and refinement of early product versions, including:
- automated clear-cut mapping
- parcel-level vegetation and time-series analytics
- bark beetle and pest detection
- thinning needs identification
- stand parameter estimation
- dashboards for forest managers and farmers
- mobile-friendly map tools for field crews
- and many others
All MVPs can be deployed and tested directly on GeoHub, fully integrated into SME workflows.
3)  Lab Testing & Model Validation
Algorithm validation using high-quality datasets from forest owners, agricultural producers, forest companies, and national inventories. Includes accuracy assessments for:
- stand parameters (species, height, diameter, biomass, stocking)
- disturbances (pests, wind, fire, excess water)
- agricultural field characteristics (water stress, excess moisture, border accuracy)
4) Industrial Pilots & Real-World Demonstrators - design and rollout of pilots with forest owners, land managers, forestry SMEs, and farmers.
BSS provides and supports ready-to-use forest monitoring services:
1. Clear-cut detection
2. Pest/disease & bark beetle monitoring
3. Windthrow, fire & excess water detection
4. Newly overgrown forest identification
5. Land-use & LULUCF change analysis
6. Stand-wise forest inventory parameter estimation
Results are delivered via GeoHub, APIs, or GIS-ready packages.
5) Full System Integration for Forestry & Agriculture Workflows
Integration of EO-based analytics into SME GIS systems (harvesting planning, risk management, certification, sustainability reporting).
Supports open standards (WMS, MVT, TMS, S3) for seamless adoption by forest and agriculture IT infrastructures.
6) Scalability Testing for Large Forest & Agriculture Estates
Performance optimisation for SMEs serving large landowners, timber companies, cooperatives, and farming groups. Includes multi-user access, fast rendering of large datasets, and automated regional-scale processing and large-scale analysis.
7) Training & Capacity Building
Expert training in remote sensing, ML-based monitoring, biomass analytics, and GeoHub-enabled workflows and operations. Supports SMEs developing green, data-driven services for sustainable forest management, precision agriculture, and circular bioeconomy applications.
8) Additional Capabilities for Forestry & Agriculture SMEs
- High-resolution drone data processing
- Integration of Sentinel-1/2, Planet, LiDAR, harvester, UAV datasets, IoT and other spatial data
- Biomass analytics and sustainability reporting
- Tools for remote stand assessment and pre-harvest valuation

column5: The Data Terra Research Institute (IR)  develops and operate a distributed digital infrastructure of data and services to observe, understand, and predict the history, functioning, and evolution of the Earth system in an integrated manner. It offers scientific communities, as well as public and private stakeholders, standardized, interoperable, and multi-source digital products and services based on data from multiple sources (satellites, in situ, soil, experiments, modeling) to discover, access, process, visualize, model, and combine data to address interdisciplinary scientific and societal challenges. Data Terra is structured around five thematic clusters and cross-cutting initiatives. Its services, designed according to the FAIR principles, integrate on-demand processing and multi-domain approaches, leveraging networked and distributed computing resources across the country.

column5:
1)	Providing R&D expertise in developing eco-sustainable building products & testing services
•	Development, optimization, and prototyping of eco-sustainable materials and components made from waste-derived aggregates and binders.
•	Mix design of normal and lightweight concretes, fibre-reinforced concretes, nano-enhanced concretes, and Phase Changing Material (PCM) modified concretes.
•	Optimization of concrete properties (rheology, workability, density, compressive strength, durability, insulation).
•	Testing on building materials:
	Geometrical, mechanical and physical characterization of aggregates
	Physical and mechanical characterization of fresh and hardened concretes and mortars (compression strength, indirect tensile strength, elastic modulus, flexural strength)
	Mechanical testing of FRC beams and plates
	Pull-off and DSS test equipment for FRP reinforcement
	Environmental chamber (1000 l)
	Thermal Conductivity Test.

column5:
Geo Engine GmbH offers services regarding geo data provisioning, time series and AI data processing, and user-centered data portals. For this, we bring and integrate our own open-source tool Geo Engine (www.geoengine.io). We also provide general consulting with respect to use cases and (data) product design, with a focus on remote sensing in general and biodiversity in particular.
Data Integration & Provisioning:
We provide SMEs with rapid access to open external data pools (e.g., Sentinel-2, ERA5, SRTM) and integrate their internal geodata into Geo Engine for uniform access. This accelerates the initial data preparation phase, allowing SMEs to focus on subject-specific issues and feasibility for a proof of concept or MVP based on EO-based services.
Example: custom locations & vegetation based on Sentinel-2
Data Processing & AI Pipelines:
We implement robust, reusable, and repeatable data and AI pipelines for processing complex geospatial data. Our expertise in geospatial time series processing helps transition innovative ideas into operational reality. Compared to simple scripting, this enables SMEs to transition more easily to a scalable service.
Example: monthly cloud-free aggregates of classified land types
Data Portal Development:
We design and build custom data portals (Web GIS, dashboards) to support either user access to data products or internal analysis and data visualization. For this, we use predefined components of our Web GIS toolbox. This helps SMEs provide data to their stakeholders more quickly.
Example: dashboard for vegetation data for the selection of property and year
General consulting:
We bring our experience in a great variety of projects with geo data in general, and biodiversity and environmental data in particular, to offer general consulting, ideation, risk management, and best practices.
Example: surface-sealing, weather effects on airports, ESG reporting

Our service areas include:
1. Ideation & Concept Development (Ideation Voucher)

column5:
CTM can provide services related to the life cycle of a mine and quarry, including systematic mapping using images and digital terrain models that allow to monitor the entire extraction process, development and research. This includes underground mining, where subsidence can be detected using radar interferometry. Regarding mineral exploration, CTM can combine geotechnical analysis with multispectral and hyperspectral images from various providers, as well as those acquired in-house using drones, tripods and laboratory equipment, to assess the raw materials present in deposits, waste rock dumps and tailings ponds. CTM can also provide results from EO for environmental, geological and hydrological risk assessments, raw material monitoring and can combine geophysical methods for subsurface investigation to analyze geological structure, locate significant ore layers, determine risks from slope instability or subsidence and conduct heritage studies.
We also specialize in using EO for geological and mineralogical studies in agriculture, applying it to various crops to determine soil composition, which is essential for good agricultural practices and its relationship to vegetation. We also conduct vegetation studies in agriculture and forestry to analyze the different stages of plant growth and detect diseases.

TerraEye supports SMEs that want to design, validate, and scale solutions based on satellite / Earth Observation (EO) and geospatial data, with a focus on raw materials, climate risk, environmental monitoring, and ESG.
Under the Ideation Voucher, we help beneficiaries identify high-value EO use cases, assess data and technical feasibility (e.g. Copernicus and commercial satellites), and define a simple concept and roadmap (what to build first, key risks, and expected impact).
Under the Scale-up Voucher, we support prototype/MVP design, KPI definition, and validation. We help structure EO data pipelines, optimise existing workflows, and integrate outputs (maps, alerts, dashboards) into the SME’s tools (GIS, BI, operational systems), with sector-specific expertise in mining and land/environment monitoring.
Under the Commercialisation Voucher, we help turn pilots into products: define the service offering, pricing logic and cost structure, packaging (APIs, dashboards, reports), and go-to-market positioning. We co-design commercial pilots, interpret EO results for non-technical stakeholders, and connect EO indicators to ESG and sustainability narratives for customers and investors.

column5:
Startup Nation Finland supports SMEs across the entire innovation lifecycle, acting as a strategic innovation intermediary that accelerates ideation, technology development, scaling, and commercialisation. Through our multidisciplinary expertise and strong ecosystem networks, we offer high-impact services aligned with the Ideation Voucher, Scale-up Voucher, and Commercialisation Voucher.
Ideation Voucher Services
We help SMEs explore new business opportunities, generate innovative concepts, and assess early feasibility. Our services include structured ideation workshops, problem definition, solution hypothesis development, user need analysis, and innovation roadmapping. We conduct initial market and competitor research, technology scouting, and benchmarking to identify viable directions. We also support SMEs in outlining preliminary product concepts, functional requirements, and collaboration pathways with universities, research labs, and ecosystem partners.
Scale-up Voucher Services
We provide support in strengthening technology, product development, and organisational capabilities for SMEs preparing for growth. This includes TRL assessment and planning, prototype development guidance, and technical advisory across hardware, IoT and software. We help refine business models, develop go-to-market strategies, and create data-driven scaling plans. SMEs receive support with supply chain readiness, industrialization processes, certification roadmaps, and IP strategy. We also enable access to strategic partners, testbeds, pilots, and international innovation networks to accelerate scaling.
Commercialisation Voucher Services
We assist SMEs in bringing validated products and services to market and securing early customers. Our services cover commercialisation strategy development, pricing and revenue modelling, customer validation, pilot planning, and sales material preparation. We support SMEs in identifying anchor customers, preparing for negotiations, and structuring market entry in Finland, the Nordics, and international markets. We also provide investor readiness support, including pitch coaching, funding strategy advisory, and preparation of investment documentation. Additional services include branding, communication strategy, and digital marketing planning to drive market adoption.
Cross-Cutting Services
Across all voucher stages, we offer strong project management support, including workplan creation, stakeholder coordination, and reporting preparation. Our deep expertise spans wireless systems, 5G/6G, photonics, optics, robotics, AR/VR/XR, digital twins, hardware prototyping, drones/BVLOS, IoT, and embedded systems. We connect SMEs with universities, research institutes, corporates, investors, and European partners, enabling cross-border collaboration and expanded innovation opportunities.
Summary
Startup Nation Finland provides SMEs with a comprehensive, end-to-end innovation service offering—from early idea generation to full commercial market entry. Our approach combines technical expertise, strategic business guidance, and access to a strong innovation ecosystem to help SMEs funded under the FIERCE programme achieve measurable growth, scalability, and real-world impact.

column5:
Kanop harnesses AI agents and satellite intelligence to de-risk nature-based investments, accelerate project development, and deliver precise climate and biodiversity impact measurements across ecosystems. We serve investors, project developers, and corporations through both our AI-powered platform and comprehensive end-to-end services.
Our solution tackles the critical measurement bottleneck that prevents scaling nature-based solutions. AI agents automatically analyze satellite imagery, radar, and LiDAR data to measure forest cover, biomass changes, biodiversity metrics, and carbon sequestration. When field measurements are available, the platform automatically calibrates satellite data to ensure scientific accuracy while cutting monitoring costs and timeframes dramatically.
We work across diverse applications: from assessing carbon project feasibility and suitability, to forecasting credit projections and monitoring restoration and protection initiatives, while also helping corporations track environmental impacts throughout their supply chains. Our capabilities support compliance with frameworks like Verra, Gold Standard, Isometric, Plan Vivo and regulations like TNFD, GHG Protocol, and CSRD.

column5: UIV Urban Innovation Vienna operates the Vienna GeoSpace Hub, an innovation lab dedicated to fostering the use of geospatial and satellite data and their integration into real-world applications. As a central interface between public administration, research, and industry, it facilitates collaboration to develop innovative and sustainable urban solutions. We support knowledge exchange and the development of concrete use cases based on societal challenges, leveraging satellite and Earth Observation (EO) data. Through events, lectures, and trend-scouting initiatives, we provide insights into the state of the art in research as well as an overview of the Austrian landscape of initiatives, startups, and companies. By offering access to our innovation network - including research institutions, public authorities, businesses, and society - we provide a wide range of collaboration opportunities. From networking events and use-case workshops to consortium building for joint projects, we support activities that lower barriers to EO adoption and accelerate the development of innovative, data-driven solutions.

We support SMEs with EUDR compliance through geolocation verification, risk scoring, and sustainability due-diligence workflows, as well as sustainability certification support such as land-use verification and hotspot identification. In addition, GRAS offers supply-chain mapping, sustainability monitoring, and customised geospatial analytics tools that help SMEs integrate Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) risk management, strengthen transparency, and scale responsible sourcing solutions. We also develop tailored analytical tools and bespoke geospatial solutions to address specific project needs and support SMEs in building innovative, data-driven sustainability strategies.

column5: AIRMO is a space-enabled climate intelligence provider specialising in verifiable methane (CH4) monitoring services. We already support companies in Europe in preparing for compliance with the EU Methane Regulation through site-level assessments using airborne platforms, including drones and aerial sensors. In parallel, AIRMO leverages Copernicus and complementary satellite data sources to detect, screen, and monitor methane emissions from space today. AIRMO is developing a next-generation satellite mission, planned for launch in 2027, designed to deliver unprecedented sensitivity and spatial resolution for detecting methane emissions below 100 kg/h. This will significantly enhance the ability to identify, quantify, and track smaller emission sources at scale. Our services are built on a multi-layer monitoring architecture that integrates satellite data, targeted airborne measurements, and robust analytics. This approach enables consistent quantification across scales, from regional hotspot identification to facility-level source attribution, while ensuring traceability and data robustness. As an External Service Provider within the FIERCE programme, AIRMO supports organisations in adopting downstream space technologies to improve operational performance, design monitoring and verification strategies, and move toward regulatory compliance and measurable environmental impact.

column5: Eyecon Group (Eyecon – Software, Lda.) provides technical development, integration, and deployment services for Earth Observation (EO)–driven digital applications. The company designs and operates cloud-native EO platforms combining satellite data (e.g. Sentinel, MODIS and Copernicus services), in-situ and IoT data streams, and AI/ML models for environmental monitoring, risk prediction, and decision-support. Eyecon Group delivers fully operational EO applications, including data ingestion pipelines, automated processing chains, predictive models, APIs, dashboards, and user-facing platforms. As an External Service Provider, Eyecon Group offers technical implementation and customisation of EO solutions such as SoilRisk (soil and land-risk monitoring), Riverflow (hydrological monitoring and flood-risk analytics), Habtrail (coastal and water-quality monitoring and early warning), as well as bespoke EO applications developed on demand. Services include EO data processing, spatial and time-series analytics, AI model training and validation, system integration with third-party platforms, and deployment on secure, scalable cloud infrastructures.

column5:
I provide system architecture and engineering services for SMEs developing complex software and AI-driven systems, with a focus on scalability, reliability, and integration with real-world operational environments.

My work operates at the system level — bridging data sources, algorithms, and physical or business processes into coherent, production-ready solutions.

Ideation Voucher (concept validation):
– Technical feasibility assessment of AI and data-driven product concepts
– Definition of system architecture aligned with business and operational goals
– Identification of key technical risks (data quality, uncertainty, system constraints)
– High-level design of MVP and delivery roadmap

Scale-up Voucher (development and system evolution):
– Architecture design for scalable, distributed, and cloud-native systems
– Integration of heterogeneous data sources (including geospatial and Earth observation data such as Copernicus)
– Transformation of raw data streams into structured, actionable signals
– Implementation of predictive and decision-support logic
– Integration with IoT and external systems where required
– Establishment of observability, monitoring, and controlled release processes

Commercialisation Voucher (stabilisation and market readiness):
– System hardening for continuous operation in production environments
– Performance optimisation and reliability engineering
– Risk analysis for AI-driven systems operating under uncertainty
– Preparation for multi-site or multi-region deployment

The services are applicable across domains such as smart infrastructure, energy systems, and AI-enabled products, particularly where software interacts with physical environments.

My experience includes over 15 years in enterprise systems, distributed architectures, and AI infrastructure, including systems operating in regulated and high-reliability contexts.

column5:
mYields provides advanced services for the development and scaling of space-enabled green solutions, combining Earth Observation (EO), geospatial analytics, cloud-based platforms, and business development expertise.

The company specializes in transforming satellite data (e.g., Sentinel), topography (DSM/SRTM), and environmental datasets into actionable insights and scalable digital services, supporting applications in agriculture, environmental monitoring, and resource management.

mYields supports SMEs across the full innovation lifecycle, from concept validation to deployment and commercialization.

Key Services
EO & Geospatial Data Integration: Integration of satellite, terrain, weather, and soil data into harmonized datasets for environmental and agricultural applications
Environmental Modelling & AI Analytics: Development of predictive models (e.g., nutrient flows, water dynamics, environmental risks) using advanced analytics and machine learning
API & Platform Development: Design and deployment of scalable SaaS APIs and cloud-native systems (AWS-based), enabling integration with third-party platforms such as Farm Management Systems (FMS)
Contactless & NFC Solutions: Development of NFC-based interaction systems for traceability, digital product passports, and real-time data access in agriculture and supply chains
Business Development & Commercialization Support: Market validation, business model design (including API-based models), and partnership development for scaling space-enabled solutions
Environmental Impact Assessment: Quantification of environmental outcomes and support for sustainability reporting aligned with EU policies
Pilot Deployment & System Integration: Implementation and validation of solutions in real-world environments, supporting TRL, IRL, and SRL advancement
Value Proposition

mYields enables organizations to convert space data and digital technologies into scalable, market-ready solutions with measurable environmental impact. By combining technical, business, and integration capabilities, mYields accelerates the adoption of space-enabled services and supports the green and digital transition across sectors.

column5: CinSOIL is a Berlin-based AgriTech and ClimateTech company, providing soil & crop monitoring service as well as regenerative agriculture & carbon farming decision support. The service, offered as SaaS, is aimed at SMEs in the agrifood sector that aim to leverage space technologies to improve the monitoring of their value chain and reduce their carbon footprint. CinSOIL’s software tool translates Earth Observation data (Sentinel 2, Landsat, and private satellite data providers) into actionable insights using ground-truth data and modelling approaches. The focus hereby is on soil carbon sequestration, aiming to enable agrifood SMEs to harness the carbon storing potential of agricultural soils as a sustainable and regenerative Scope 3 decarbonisation strategy. In addition to the monitoring service, CinSOIL offers a decision support tool that provides tailored recommendations on regenerative agricultural practices to agrifood companies and their partner farmers. While the preferred implementation area is arable land (food grain, wheat supply chain), the service is also available for other land use types.

column1: Universidad Carlos III de Madrid (Laboratorio de Sensores Teledetección e Imagen en el Infrarrojo)
column5:
The Infrared Laboratory (LIR) at Universidad Carlos III de Madrid (UC3M) has an experience of more than 30 years in applied infrared sensing, including environmental monitoring (e.g. methane emissions), combustion diagnostics, and industrial applications, as well as strong links with industry. We offer advanced scientific and engineering services in the field of infrared (IR) sensors and imaging. Our expertise spans from fundamental radiometry and detector characterization to the development and validation of application-oriented IR measurement systems.
As an External Service Provider (ESP), we can support SMEs through the following services:

1. Assessment of IR-based sensing solutions for specific applications (e.g. gas detection, thermal monitoring, process control), through:
•	Identification of optimal spectral bands (MWIR/LWIR) and sensor technologies
•	Radiative transfer modelling and simulation
•	Performance estimation (detectability, sensitivity, NETD, spectral requirements) 

2. System design and optimisation 
•	Definition of IR system architectures 
•	Trade-off analysis (cost vs performance, SWaP constraints) 
•	Support in the selection and integration of IR cameras and multi-and hyper-spectral systems. 
•	Design of measurement methodologies adapted to industrial environments 

3. Algorithm development and data processing
•	Development of advanced signal and image processing methods for IR data 
•	Hyperspectral data analysis (feature extraction, classification, anomaly detection) 
•	Quantitative Optical Gas Imaging (QOGI): algorithm development for gas detection and quantification 
•	Integration of physics-based models (radiative transfer) with data-driven approaches 

4. Experimental validation and performance assessment 
•	Design and execution of controlled experiments (laboratory and field)
•	Validation of detection/quantification performance under realistic conditions
•	Radiometric calibration of IR instrumentation, emissivity measurements, and  uncertainty analysis.
•	Multispectral and hyperspectral measurements in the MWIR and LWIR bands.
•	Active thermography for NDT
•	Support for standardisation and regulatory compliance, including definition of performance metrics and test protocols and preparation of technical documentation to support certification and market adoption 

5. Training and knowledge transfer
•	Tailored training for SME personnel on IR imaging, thermography, and gas detection 
•	Workshops on hyperspectral imaging and quantitative analysis 
•	Support for building in-house capabilities

Improved Environmental Monitoring
With over 25 years of experience, Remote Sensing Solutions (RSS) is one of Germany’s leading experts in Earth observation. We actively shape the future of remote sensing—from data science to the development of fully automated processing chains. By leveraging state-of-the-art technologies, we set the highest standards in operational environmental monitoring.
RSS offers comprehensive, end-to-end solutions: from precise data analysis to customized cloud applications (Software as a Service), making complex geodata immediately accessible and actionable for our clients.
We transform complex satellite and aerial imagery into precise, digital foundations for decision-making in nature conservation, climate protection, and global environmental challenges. By combining scientific expertise with cutting-edge geoinformatics, we support government agencies, corporations, and NGOs worldwide in utilizing natural resources sustainably and making climate protection goals measurable.

column5:
Moss Hub Ltd. offers specialised services at the intersection of nature-based solutions, environmental monitoring, and data-driven urban innovation, supporting SMEs in integrating space-derived environmental data into practical applications.
The company provides expertise in air quality assessment, including the design and deployment of monitoring systems combining ground-based sensors with satellite-derived data (e.g. Copernicus Earth Observation services). Services include identification of environmental hotspots, spatial analysis, and validation of environmental performance.
Moss Hub also supports the development and testing of nature-based solutions (NbS), including moss-based biofilters, for urban environments. This includes pilot design, installation guidance, performance monitoring, and impact assessment (e.g. particulate matter reduction, microclimate effects).
In addition, the company offers integration support between environmental data and physical interventions, enabling SMEs to optimise solution placement, improve efficiency, and demonstrate measurable impact.
With a strong research background and collaboration network across Europe, Moss Hub ensures high-quality, scientifically grounded, and application-oriented services tailored to SME innovation projects.

column5:
Flahtik is a technology company specializing in Geospatial Intelligence, Artificial Intelligence, Environmental Research, and Digital Solutions. We combine GIS, Remote Sensing, GeoAI, and full-stack Web & Mobile development to help SMEs design, build, and deploy space-enabled green solutions, from early concept to market-ready product.

- Ideation Voucher, Feasibility & Concept Development
Flahtik helps SMEs define and validate their green innovation concept. We conduct geospatial feasibility studies, identify the most relevant Earth Observation (EO) and satellite data sources, and translate environmental challenges into clear, data-driven solution roadmaps, combining GIS analysis with AI-assisted insights and digital prototyping.

- Scale-up Voucher – Development & Testing
We provide end-to-end technical development services including satellite imagery processing (Sentinel, Landsat, MODIS), GeoAI model development for environmental monitoring, custom web and mobile application development, and interactive geospatial dashboards. We build, test, and iterate solutions that are robust, scalable, and ready for real-world use.

- Commercialisation Voucher – Market-Ready Deployment
Flahtik delivers production-grade, fully integrated digital solutions. We develop scalable GIS web platforms, AI-powered environmental monitoring systems, cross-platform mobile applications, and automated EO data pipelines, all backed by full technical documentation, user training, and long-term support.

* Core Services Offered:
GIS mapping, spatial analysis & advanced cartography
EO & satellite data processing and interpretation
GeoAI & machine learning for environmental applications
Environmental impact assessment & climate risk analysis
Full-stack web application development
Cross-platform mobile app development (iOS & Android)
Interactive dashboards & data visualization platforms
Custom decision-support tools & geospatial portals
Land use monitoring, biodiversity & ecosystem analysis
Research support & scientific reporting

Flahtik empowers SMEs with the full technology stack they need, from space data intelligence to user-facing digital products, turning environmental data into measurable, scalable impact.

Are you a FIERCE programme beneficiary? HowToTransform AB is External Service Provider.
If you've received an Ideation Voucher, Scale-Up Voucher, or Commercialisation Voucher under the FIERCE programme and you need expert support to turn your green solution into a fundable, scalable business, this is for you.

Who we are
HowToTransform AB is a Swedish venture architecture and commercialisation firm led by Abir Hossain, a deep-tech venture builder and innovation operator with 15+ years of experience supporting green-tech and industrial SMEs across Europe.
We've supported 40+ scale-ups, closed 9 investment deals, and enabled €60M+ in co-funding, working across Sweden, Finland, the Baltics, the UK, Central Europe, and beyond.

What we offer FIERCE beneficiaries
🔵 Ideation Voucher (up to €25,000)
→ Green market & competitive intelligence
→ Business model design
→ Stakeholder co-design & funding architecture
→ Seed funding readiness & access
🟢 Scale-Up Voucher (up to €50,000)
→ Market–product fit validation
→ Revenue stream validation
→ Key partnership development & corporate introductions
→ Early-stage business planning + incubation pathway advisory
🔴 Commercialisation Voucher (up to €25,000)
→ Full investor-grade business planning
→ KPI framework & commercial dashboard design
→ Partner & clientele development for market entry
→ VC & business angel investment readiness + warm introductions

What outcomes look like in practice
✅ €2.8M investment round closed - DREV (cleantech, critical metals recovery) via curated VC introductions to Butterfly Ventures + Almi Invest GreenTech
✅ EIT Venture Award 2025, Top 3 - DigiClean Solutions AB, supported from concept → spinout → strategic investment → commercial deployment
✅ Corporate funded PoC with Roca and Helmee Imaging (Finnish scaleup), following corporate introductions at Advanced Factories Barcelona
✅ €1M equity investment, Oak Network (energy efficiency), including IKEA engagement and 11 corporate connections
✅ €1M+ equity round, 7 investor introductions for Stilride/Stilfold (advanced manufacturing, EIT Venture Award finalist)
✅ 20+ manufacturing corporate introductions per engagement to Viking Analytics, via Access2Tech Europe - a framework to bring industries, solution providers, investors together. 
✅ €250K Nordic Climate Facility grant - Juteborg / Farmer to Factory™ sustainable materials supply chain

Our approach
We don't produce generic slide decks or boilerplate reports.
Every engagement is structured, time-bounded, and evidence-driven designed to produce decision-grade outputs that work for investors, grant evaluators, and commercial partners.

Idea → validated business model → right partners → fundable plan → investor conversations that convert.

That is the arc we are built to support.
